title: Phlegmariurus obtusifolius
intro: Phlegmariurus obtusifolius, commonly known as the Chinese forked schisandra vine, is a species of climbing shrub in the family Lycopodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Phlegmariurus obtusifolius include lignans, alkaloids, and terpenes, which have been shown to exhibit potent neuroprotective, anti-cancer, and anti-viral activities. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phlegmariurus obtusifolius is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including coughs, sore throats, and digestive disorders.
